item.title: Eni gets Nigeria go-ahead to invest $10.3 billion in deepwater projects
item.description: Long-delayed Zabazaba and Etan developments could be online in 2029, according to NUPRC executives

item.title: Kuwait extends force majeure on oil exports - report
item.description: Risk to shipping via Strait of Hormuz prompts decision by Kuwait Petroleum Corporation

item.title: Shell faces new court case seeking ban on all new oil and gas developments
item.description: Shell defeated a climate case against the Dutch climate group Milieudefensie in 2024

item.title: Vaalco gains momentum in Gabon drilling campaign
item.description: Latest well in Gabon brought onstream after hitting 'better-than-expected' reservoir

item.title: Bidding line-up revealed for Indian licensing rounds offering 16 blocks
item.description: India pushing ahead with oil and gas bid rounds to reduce dependence on crude oil imports and secure long-term energy security

item.title: ‘Trust is hard to heal’: Breakdown in global trade and supply chains likely permanent
item.description: Modern trading system built around multilateral rules and just‑in‑time efficiency is replaced by insular world characterised by regional blocs, higher costs and thinner margins

item.title: Australian opposition vows to fast-track oil and gas project approvals
item.description: Incentives also on the table for early-stage exploration

item.title: Second potentially play-opening well on the cards in PNG
item.description: Larus Energy looking to raise $100 million for 2027 Nanamarope exploration well

item.title: COOEC installs jacket for South China Sea oilfield
item.description: New wellhead platform to support production at Weizhou fields

item.title: Middle East conflict takes toll on leading Australian contractor
item.description: Worley expects multimillion-dollar hit as project schedules derailed and new awards slow

item.title: Middle East project set to draw strong contractor interest
item.description: Oman's state-owned OQ envisages developing NGL extraction facilities at Saih Nihayda with transportation to Duqm, where the liquids will be fractionated and stored for export

item.title: Markets failed to assess scale of Iran conflict's disruption to energy, says Citadel executive
item.description: Surge in volatility forced several traders to cut positions even as arbitrage opportunities multiplied

item.title: Equinor fires up new Bay du Nord subsea tender process
item.description: Equinor begins hunt for more than 150 kilometres of hardware for big subsea system

item.title: Is Orsted next on the block as Equinor sells renewables stake?
item.description: Analysts ponder implications for oil giant's holding in Danish offshore wind group as it divests half of its shares in Scatec

item.title: 'Wind farm' built on Donald Trump's favourite golf course
item.description: Greenpeace campaigners storm one of US president's Scottish courses to protest against his energy policy

item.title: Canadian player boosts gas production by a third at heavily depleted field
item.description: Condor Energies completes fourth productive well at Kumli field in Uzbekistan

item.title: TotalEnergies CEO Pouyanne rules out three more offshore wind markets after US exit
item.description: Patrick Pouyanne's latest blow to offshore wind risks leading sector to concentrate risk in only a handful of countries, says industry expert

item.title: Trump budget cuts threaten US petroleum geoscience research
item.description: Federal government's science spending cuts have left huge gaps in oil and gas research that industry is not filling

item.title: Algeria launches fresh exploration bid round
item.description: Seven exploration areas are slated for award later this year

item.title: Japanese company makes new play in Norway
item.description: Mistral South discovery has been linked to potential development with Linnorm gas field

item.title: Norwegian driller halts operations after dropping blowout preventer
item.description: Odfjell working to recover BOP that dropped from the Deepsea Atlantic

item.title: Eni eyes third production hub after latest giant discovery in Indonesia’s prolific Kutei basin
item.description: Preliminary estimates indicate Geliga-1 has 5 trillion cubic feet of in-place gas resources that could underpin fast-track project

item.title: Harbour hot on trail of Mexico FPSO solutions
item.description: UK-based operator is developing two big oilfields in parallel

item.title: Petronas suspends production at Iraqi oilfield
item.description: Shipments from Garraf field halted with ‘no prospect of resumption’

item.title: Oil prices surge as US-Iran hostilities flare up
item.description: Strait of Hormuz effectively closed again after weekend attacks on vessels

item.title: Energy security fires up Australia exploration push
item.description: Discovery of new oilfield described as 'golden opportunity' amid supply security unease, but efforts to boost domestic output attract strong green pushback

item.title: All eyes on contractors laying major Vietnam pipeline system
item.description: Major infrastructure project to transport offshore gas to onshore gas-to-electricity complex

item.title: Asian giant abruptly cancels keenly-watched offshore rig tender, relaunches within days
item.description: ONGC is responsible for almost three-quarters of India’s crude oil output and is spending heavily on exploration and development to maintain and further boost its production

item.title: Iran shuts down Strait of Hormuz again, demanding end to US blockade
item.description: Crucial waterway returns to its ‘previous state’, say Iran’s armed forces

item.title: US Supreme Court sides with Chevron in $745 million coastal lawsuit
item.description: Unanimous ruling opens door for case to be retried in federal venue
